_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Bladder injury repair with colostomy (with or without urethral injury)
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB or equivalent in (Urology, Pediatric Surgery)
clinical_indications: Hematuria invariably accompanies bladder injury. Patients often present with the triad of gross hematuria, suprapubic pain or tenderness, and difficulty urinating or inability to void.
Gross hematuria does not always occur, however; in approximately 10% of cases, the hematuria is microscopic.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
Cystogram report confirming the bladder injury

claim.label: Post procedure photograph with an evidence of colostomy created

ppd_preauth.q: Was the USG / CT scan report suggestive of bladder injury?

cpd_claim.q: Were the detailed Operative notes / procedure notes submitted?

common_queries: USG / CT scan report not suggestive of bladder injury.
Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management are missing or incomplete.
USG / CT scan / MRI report is missing or inconclusive.
Cystogram report confirming bladder injury is missing or inconclusive.
Post procedure photograph with an evidence of colostomy created is missing.
Detailed Operative notes / procedure notes are missing or lack necessary detail.

clinical_key_pointers: Bruising at the site of injury
In trauma situations, blood at the urethral meatus is an absolute indication for retrograde urethrography.
Incase patients with posterior urethral injury associated bladder injury it is critical that no attempt at blind passage of a urethral catheter is made.
